Package org.eventb.internal.pp.core.inferrers

Class CaseSplitInferrer

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.pp.core.inferrers.AbstractInferrer
org.eventb.internal.pp.core.inferrers.CaseSplitInferrer
All Implemented Interfaces:
IInferrer
public class CaseSplitInferrer extends AbstractInferrer
This class is responsible for splitting a clause in two.

For now, it also contains the logic that chooses where the clause is split. TODO This logic should move in the CaseSplitter.

    • inferFromDisjunctiveClauseHelper

      protected void inferFromDisjunctiveClauseHelper(Clause clause)
      Description copied from class: AbstractInferrer
      Infers from a disjunctive clause.

      This method is called just before reset(). When this method returns, the result of the inferrence must be ready.

      Specified by:
      inferFromDisjunctiveClauseHelper in class AbstractInferrer

    • inferFromEquivalenceClauseHelper

      protected void inferFromEquivalenceClauseHelper(Clause clause)
      Description copied from class: AbstractInferrer
      Infers from an equivalence clause.

      This method is called just before reset(). When this method returns, the result of the inferrence must be ready.

      Specified by:
      inferFromEquivalenceClauseHelper in class AbstractInferrer

    • initialize

      protected void initialize(Clause clause) throws IllegalStateException
      Description copied from class: AbstractInferrer
      Initializes the inferrer with the specified clause.

      This method is called right before the fields have been populated with the literals of the clause. When this method is called, all original literals of the clause can be accessed, and manipulated (some of them can be removed, for instance). After this method returns, the remaining literal lists are copied.

      This method is also the right place to throw an exception if the inferrer is not initialized properly.

      Specified by:
      initialize in class AbstractInferrer
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException

      protected void reset()
      Overrides:
